name: Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares from the collado de Fuente del Chivo
name_original: Peña Labra y Pico Tres Mares desde el collado de Fuente del Chivo
region_intro: Hiking around Polaciones offers diverse landscapes in the western part of Cantabria, characterized by impressive mountain ranges and extensive forests. The region features peaks exceeding 2,000 meters, including Peña Sagra, Pico Tres Mares, and Peña Labra. Lush beech and oak forests are prevalent, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Polaciones also encompasses protected natural areas, contributing to its rich biodiversity and well-preserved rural environment. Best hiking trails around Polaciones The most popular hiking route is Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares from the collado de Fuente del Chivo, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends prominent peaks, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Ciquera from the collado de Piedrasluengas — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail explores the natural park, featuring varied terrain and scenic vistas. Local hikers also love the Pico Tres Mares y Peña Labra desde collado de Piedrasluengas — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through mountain landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Polaciones is defined by towering peaks, lush forests, and serene river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Polaciones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Polaciones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Polaciones?
A: Polaciones is characterized by its stunning natural beauty, featuring majestic mountain ranges, expansive forests, and a well-preserved rural environment. You'll encounter towering peaks like Peña Sagra, Pico Tres Mares, and Peña Labra, often exceeding 2,000 meters. The landscape also boasts lush beech and oak forests, serene river valleys, and green hills, offering diverse scenery for your hikes.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Polaciones?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails in Polaciones, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 500 times, indicating their popularity and accessibility.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Polaciones offers several easy hiking trails. There are 6 routes classified as easy, perfect for those looking for a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak – Tres Mares loop from Estación de Esquí y Montaña Alto Campoo&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.8 km long and takes less than an hour to complete.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Polaciones offers several breathtaking viewpoints and cultural landmarks. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views of the Picos de Europa, Peña Labra, and Pico Tres Mares. You can also discover historical elements like the ruins of an old mill, traditional fountains, and even an ancient wolf corral near Lombraña.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Polaciones?
A: Yes, Polaciones features several circular routes that allow you to start and end in the same location, often connecting traditional villages. For example, routes link villages like Pejanda, Belmonte, Cotillos, and Santa Eulalia, or Lombraña, Tresabuela, and Uznayo, providing a blend of natural beauty and rural charm.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Polaciones?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, including lush forests and high peaks, are enjoyable across seasons. Spring and autumn offer vibrant colors in the beech and oak forests, while summer provides pleasant conditions for higher mountain ascents. Winter can be challenging in the high mountains but offers unique snowy vistas.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Polaciones?
A: Polaciones is rich in biodiversity, being part of protected natural areas. You might spot wild boars, deer, and if you're very lucky, the emblematic Cantabrian brown bear. The forests are also home to various birds like treecreepers, nuthatches, tits, and woodpeckers.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, Polaciones offers challenging routes that require good physical condition. There are 3 difficult routes available, often involving ascents to prominent peaks. These trails provide rewarding experiences with expansive views from high altitudes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Polaciones?
A: The hiking trails in Polaciones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountain views, the tranquility of the forests, and the well-preserved natural environment.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the natural parks in the area?
A: Absolutely. A significant portion of Polaciones falls within protected natural spaces, including the Zona de Especial Conservación (ZEC) Valles Altos del Nansa y Saja y Alto Campoo. You can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068172647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ciquera from the collado de Piedrasluengas — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through these ecologically important areas.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to the highest peaks in Polaciones?
A: Yes, several routes lead to the impressive peaks surrounding Polaciones. You can hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt;, which offer spectacular panoramic views. Routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996923174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares from the collado de Fuente del Chivo&lt;/a&gt; are popular choices for reaching these summits.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Polaciones?
A: Moderate hikes in Polaciones vary in length and duration. For example, a route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068176113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tres Mares y Peña Labra desde collado de Piedrasluengas — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 12.8 km, typically takes around 4 hours and 47 minutes to complete.
tips: At 2029 meters above sea level, the top of Peña Labra is located in a privileged place. It represents the border between the Community of Cantabria and Castilla y León, so to the north you will have impressive views of the Cantabrian interior and the mountains of the Picos de Europa National Park, while to the south you will be able to appreciate another wonderful landscape such as the of the Montaña Palencia Natural Park.

Very close to the Alto Campo ski resort and located at 2,149 meters above sea level, the top of Tres Mares is one of those peaks that you cannot miss. You will have wonderful views towards the Cantabrian interior, the Picos de Europa National Park and the neighboring Montaña Palentina Natural Park in the Castilla y León region.
